page: Amazon Web Services m8a.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes support for advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8a.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across compute, memory, and specialized workloads. It achieves top-decile results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as PassMark CPU and memory suites. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers high bandwidth, exceeding 400 GB/sec for large-block read operations. This high throughput translates to exceptional performance in data-intensive tasks, including static web serving, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

The instance also exhibits strong capabilities in local artificial intelligence workloads, processing LLM inference prompts and text generation efficiently across small, medium, and large models. However, the instance does not include local NVMe storage or discrete GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached volumes over its 30 Gbps baseline network interface. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-dense, compute-heavy applications that do not require local ephemeral storage, such as relational databases, enterprise application hosting, and containerized microservices.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz and 256 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier performance in CPU and memory benchmarks, including stress-ng, PassMark, and Redis operations. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, but lacks local storage and discrete GPUs, relying instead on a 30 Gbps baseline network interface for external storage connectivity. The balanced 4 GB of RAM per core makes it highly efficient for enterprise workloads, database hosting, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 30 Gbps network interface, delivering top-tier compute and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-performance general-purpose virtual server featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
Features a 30 Gbps baseline network interface with no local storage
Optimized for enterprise applications, databases, and LLM inference
